title: NOC 51113 – Journalists | GTR Immigration
description: Journalists under NOC 51113 are professional media specialists responsible for researching, investigating, writing, and reporting news and feature stories across Canada. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation typically requires a university degree in journalism or communications. Journalists are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5111 – Writing, translating and related communications professionalsNOC Code51113

duties: Journalists perform some or all of the following duties:
Collect local, national, and international news through interviews and investigation
Write news stories for publication and broadcast
Analyze and verify news content for accuracy
Conduct interviews for radio and television programs
Research and report on specialized topics such as science, medicine, and technology
Prepare feature columns and in-depth reports
Produce critical reviews of artistic and cultural works
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in journalism, communications, or a related field is usually required
Practical experience through internships or freelance work is often preferred
Strong writing and investigative skills are essential
salary_prospects: $38,900 – $92,600 per year,depending on experience, specialization, and employer type.
job_outlook: Expansion of digital and multimedia news
Opportunities are strongest in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
NOC 51113 is classified under TEER 1, making it generally eligible under:
Eligibility depends on education credential assessment (ECA), language proficiency, and skilled work experience.

labour_market_demand: Expansion of multimedia and broadcast journalism
Replacement demand due to workforce turnover
Employment prospects are considered stable, with balanced supply and demand.
Opportunities in print, broadcast, and digital media
Roles in investigative and specialized reporting
Journalists play a crucial role in informing the public and maintaining transparency in Canadian society.
